danger_signals.visual_form: Character models of Poseur Pete, Aggro Eddie, and Bionic Lester with distinct visual identities
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; hand-to-hand knockout competition represents escalating danger based on skill difficulty

danger_signals.visual_form: Park and street settings with terrain obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards and collision risks inherent to downhill progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Enclosed pool environment with opponent skaters
danger_signals.meaning: Confined space creates tactical danger and unavoidable confrontation

safety_signals.visual_form: Rodney Recloose's skateshop operated by the purple-mohawked character
safety_signals.meaning: Hub location representing safety, commerce, and legitimate skateboarding culture

safety_signals.visual_form: Half-pipe arena with no opponents present
safety_signals.meaning: Safe performance space without direct competitive threat
Safe skill demonstration through individual achievement rather than combat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Skateshop ownership; Marine Corps tattoo signifying disciplined culture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Skateshop hub; organized events in designated venues (parks, pools, ramps)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Lester and his 'cronies' (Poseur Pete, Aggro Eddie) form rival group
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Poseur Pete (beginner-tier threat), Aggro Eddie (intermediate-tier threat), Bionic Lester (advanced-tier threat with two supporting cronies)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Street settings (downhill jam); pool arenas; confrontational environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Poseur Pete appears as opponent in joust; easier downhill/pool challenges
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Less aggressive opponent behavior; reduced difficulty in head-to-head events

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aggro Eddie replaces Poseur Pete as opponent; moderate difficulty scaling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: More challenging skateboarding mechanics; faster opponent movements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bionic Lester appears with two supporting cronies for the joust event
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Highest difficulty; multi-opponent confrontation in pool joust

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Up to eight players visible in character select; multiple event venues displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression through five different skateboarding events; cumulative scoring across tournament

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban environment distinct from park or pool arenas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Head-to-head competitive racing format; street-culture aesthetic

environmental_storytelling.example: Rodney Recloose's purple mohawk and Marine Corps tattoo establish him as authoritative father-figure in skateboarding culture; Bionic Lester's green flattop and 'wild' descriptor position him as rebellious youth counterpart
Park vs. street settings communicate cultural context: parks represent organized, sanctioned venues while streets represent grassroots, competitive culture
Rodney runs the shop (business/authority); Lester is the tournament opponent (rival/challenge); their relationship communicated through role separation
Lester's two supporting characters (Poseur Pete and Aggro Eddie) visually establish him as leader of a rival skateboarding faction
Pool joust being 'hand-to-hand knockout competition' versus ramp events being point-based suggests contrast between street fighting culture and legitimate sporting merit

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Operates skateshop; facilitates tournament access; positioned as father figure managing legitimate skateboarding scene
Appears as final joust opponent for advanced players; commands cronies (Poseur Pete, Aggro Eddie); represents peak competitive difficulty
Challenges novice players in joust; lower skill expression; functions as entry-level threat
Challenges intermediate players; escalated aggression compared to Pete; positioned between Pete and Lester in progression
Can face off against all three opponents in joust based on skill level; competes across five different event types; progression determines opponent encounters

notes: Skate or Die! employs minimal but effective visual faction signaling through character design (mohawk colors, attire) and venue differentiation. The world-state system is skill-based progression rather than environmental/political conditions—difficulty tiers directly map to opponent identity. The game communicates 'danger' through opponent characterization (Pete→Eddie→Lester progression) rather than environmental hazards. Street vs. park venue distinction carries implicit cultural meaning: parks as legitimate venues, streets as raw/competitive spaces. The family relationship between Rodney and Lester provides narrative framing for the legitimate vs. wild skateboarding divide. Limited visual detail in NES version constrains manifestation complexity; character colors and arena types carry primary communication weight.
